justification: All 5 rubric problems identified: (1) Connection pool math performed — 96/100 explicitly stated with death spiral analysis; (2) Synchronous call chain identified as 4-hop cascading failure risk with latency amplification; (3) Redis SPOF clearly called out; (4) Shared CI/CD identified as defeating microservices independence; (5) NFS log mount identified as hidden shared dependency with I/O contention risk. Prioritized 3-phase remediation plan provided with sequencing appropriate for a small team. Trade-offs documented for all 5 major decisions with alternatives considered.

observations.text: Connection pool math explicitly performed: 8×12=96 of 100, with insight that 4-hop chain holds multiple connections simultaneously
NFS correctly identified as hidden shared dependency with synchronous write blocking risk
Shared CI/CD explicitly called out as defeating independent deployability — the primary benefit of microservices

3-phase remediation plan is well-sequenced: immediate safety nets (circuit breakers, PgBouncer), then decoupling (async notifications, JWT, pipeline split), then deeper restructuring (database-per-service, sagas)
Trade-offs are thorough with gains, sacrifices, and alternatives for each decision
Excellent pitfalls_to_avoid section shows deep understanding of migration risks
Identified additional risks beyond the 5 required: hidden data coupling, latency amplification, no backpressure
Call chain described as 4-hop but scenario specifies 5-hop (Gateway -> Order -> Courier -> Carrier API -> Notification) — MJ counted 4 arrows but the chain involves 5 services
Could have been more explicit about the 9th service scaling scenario — the rubric emphasizes that adding one more service or scaling any existing service to 2 instances will break the pool

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"This architecture exhibits several critical structural problems that will compound under growth. The single PostgreSQL instance with near-saturated connection pools creates a hard scalability ceiling and single point of failure. A 4-hop synchronous call chain (API Gateway → Order → Courier → Carrier API → Notification) creates cascading failure risk and latency amplification. Shared Redis for auth, shared NFS for logs, and a monolithic deployment pipeline mean these 8 'micr
